tasks.description: Runbook instances [1/3] — data layer: migration 016 (runbooks + runbook_steps tables, enums, RLS, indexes, triggers), TypeScript types (RunbookStatus, RunbookStepStatus, Runbook, RunbookStep), API routes (GET/POST /runbooks, GET/PATCH/DELETE /runbooks/[id], GET /runbook-steps?runbook_id=, PATCH /runbook-steps/[id]), client wrappers. POST /runbooks snapshot-copies section steps into runbook_steps.
acceptance_criteria: Migration 016_runbooks.sql creates enums runbook_status (active/completed/cancelled) and runbook_step_status (pending/in_progress/completed/skipped)

runbooks table: id, workspace_id (FK), section_id (FK), name, status (runbook_status DEFAULT active), started_at (DEFAULT now()), completed_at (nullable), created_by (FK users), created_at, updated_at

runbook_steps table: id, runbook_id (FK CASCADE), step_id (FK SET NULL — original step reference), status (runbook_step_status DEFAULT pending), assigned_to (FK users nullable), completed_at (nullable), notes (TEXT nullable), position (INTEGER), created_at, updated_at
RLS on both tables via can_access_workspace() — runbooks has workspace_id directly, runbook_steps joins through runbook
Indexes: runbooks(workspace_id), runbook_steps(runbook_id), runbook_steps(runbook_id, position)
updated_at triggers on both tables
TypeScript types in database.ts: RunbookStatus, RunbookStepStatus enums, Runbook and RunbookStep interfaces
GET /api/v1/runbooks?workspace_id= returns Runbook[] — list for workspace
POST /api/v1/runbooks with { workspace_id, section_id, name } — creates runbook AND fetches section's steps ordered by position, bulk-inserts as runbook_steps
GET /api/v1/runbooks/[id] returns single Runbook
PATCH /api/v1/runbooks/[id] updates name, status, completed_at
DELETE /api/v1/runbooks/[id] deletes runbook (CASCADE deletes runbook_steps)
GET /api/v1/runbook-steps?runbook_id= returns RunbookStep[] ordered by position
PATCH /api/v1/runbook-steps/[id] updates status, assigned_to, completed_at, notes
Client wrappers: fetchRunbooks(workspaceId), createRunbook(data), fetchRunbook(id), updateRunbook(id, data), deleteRunbook(id), fetchRunbookSteps(runbookId), updateRunbookStep(id, data)

patterns_to_follow: Migration pattern: CREATE TABLE + trigger + indexes + RLS (see 015_tasks.sql)
API envelope: successResponse(data)/errorResponse(code, msg) from lib/api/response.ts
Client wrapper: apiFetch<T>() returns T directly — no nested access
RLS: can_access_workspace(workspace_id) for tables with workspace_id. For runbook_steps: RLS via subquery joining runbooks to get workspace_id
POST create pattern: validate required fields, insert, return created entity
POST /runbooks special: after creating runbook, query steps WHERE section_id = X ORDER BY position, bulk-insert as runbook_steps with same position values
learnings_relevant: apiFetch unwraps envelope — client functions return apiFetch<T>() directly
Supabase RLS silent mutations — check if data is null after mutations, return 403
API route HTTP methods: sections/stages are POST-only at collection (no GET list). Runbooks SHOULD have GET list.

tasks.special_instructions: POST /runbooks is the key endpoint: creates runbook AND snapshot-copies section's steps. Use Supabase server client to fetch steps WHERE section_id = req.section_id ORDER BY position ASC. Insert runbook_steps with step_id as reference to original step (for linking back to canvas), but the runbook checklist is independent — status changes on runbook_steps do NOT affect original steps. runbook_steps.step_id should be FK with ON DELETE SET NULL (if original step is deleted, runbook step remains as orphaned checklist item). The name field on runbook should default to section.name if not provided.

handleAddStage wrapped in useCallback with appropriate dependency array
Both added to handleKeyDown useCallback dependency array if not already
ESLint exhaustive-deps warnings for these handlers resolved
No new lint warnings introduced
npx tsc --noEmit passes

patterns_to_follow: IMP-008 fix: handleAddStep and handleAddSection in flow-canvas.tsx were wrapped in useCallback and added to handleKeyDown's dependency array (iter 78, commit e0e95d8)

tasks.special_instructions: Read flow-canvas.tsx to see the exact IMP-008 pattern. Apply the same useCallback wrapping to handleAddTouchpoint (line ~345) and handleAddStage (line ~363) in journey-canvas-view.tsx. Verify the dependency arrays are correct for each handler.
